source: owner-delivered in-chat 2026-07-14 (verbatim rules + budgets; owner stance: 'not a big fan of unlimited but dont like to restrict i need a good standard')
policy_note: Recipe/policy data, never identity. The standard separates three capacities: GENERATION (bounded by budgets), STORAGE (candidates + generated aliases retained in provisional index), RETRIEVAL USAGE (only promoted/top-ranked per query mode). Budgets are operational, not permanent schema limits — they control promotion and per-query usage, not what may exist.
definition: A latent concept is an underlying semantic abstraction inferred from one or more claims, even when the source never names it directly. Explicit concept: 'reference price'. Latent concept: 'repeated external signals reshape internal valuation baselines'.

creation_process.step_1_python_detects: Python examines accepted claims, predicates, relations, and superframes; emits an UNNAMED structural candidate: {pattern_id, supporting_claim_ids[], abstract_roles[], frame_sequence[]}
creation_process.step_2_llm_names: The parent-summary LLM call receives the detected pattern and proposes {preferred_label, definition, supporting_claim_ids[]}
creation_process.step_3_python_normalizes: Python normalizes, dedupes/clusters into concept families (originals retained as generated aliases), stores as candidate with assignment_state
creation_process.interim_v1_note: Until the claim layer exists, Ghost A emits latent candidates directly from parent text (derivation_method=llm_proposal, supporting_claim_ids=[]). These remain CANDIDATES; when claim-grounded detection (v2) lands, v1 candidates are corroborated->promoted or remain provisional. Consistent with the permission ladder — no wasted capture.

scope_levels: cross-corpus bridge (generated lazily after indexing; most valuable for polymath-style retrieval)

reject_or_merge_if_only: domain label disguised as a concept

alias_clustering: Python clusters near-duplicate candidates into canonical concept families; the raw candidates persist as generated_aliases on the family (they feed query-vocabulary matching); only canonical families power retrieval seats.

domain_vs_latent.domain: classified field of knowledge — answers 'in what body of knowledge does this belong'
domain_vs_latent.adjacent_domain: a field the passage meaningfully touches without using its vocabulary — still a DOMAIN classification
domain_vs_latent.latent_concept: the inferred idea underneath the claims — may be domain-local, cross-domain, document-level, parent-level, or a cross-corpus bridge
